extra.reasoning: rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (IF asthma). rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, whose 10-term conjunction INCLUDES asthma. Therefore any input satisfying r2 necessarily also satisfies r1. The two rules write to different outputs (r1->rec_drug_b, r2->rec_drug_a), so per-output priority/order conflict resolution never makes them compete; each output independently takes its firing rule, and no rule ever sets either output false. Setting all 10 r2 conditions true (asthma, peptic_ulcer, elderly, qt_prolongation, bradycardia, low_ejection_fraction, anticoagulated, renal_impairment, heart_failure, immunosuppressed) fires both r1 and r2, yielding rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously. ckd_dialysis only affects rec_drug_c (r3) and is irrelevant; set false for minimality. This jointly satisfies (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b), violating the mutual-exclusion property.
